tool.name: Opsin Phenotype Tool for Inference of Color Sensitivity (OPTICS)
tool.description: Predicts λmax of opsins based amino-acid sequences
tool.command:
python '$__tool_directory__/optics_galaxy_wrapper.py' --input '$input' --prediction_prefix '$prediction_prefix' --model '$model' --encoding '$encoding' --n_jobs '$advanced_options.n_jobs' #if $advanced_options.tolerate_non_standard_aa --tolerate_non_standard_aa #end if
## BLASTp Options #if $blastp_group.blastp --blastp --refseq $blastp_group.refseq_conditional.refseq #if str($blastp_group.refseq_conditional.refseq) == "custom" --custom_ref_file '$blastp_group.refseq_conditional.ref_seq_file' #end if --output_blastp '$output_blastp' #else --output_blastp 'null' #end if
## Bootstrap Options #if $bootstrap_group.bootstrap --bootstrap --bootstrap_num 100
#if $bootstrap_group.viz_options.visualize_bootstrap --visualize_bootstrap --save_viz_as $bootstrap_group.viz_options.save_viz_as #if $bootstrap_group.viz_options.full_spectrum_xaxis --full_spectrum_xaxis #end if --output_bootstrap_viz '$output_bootstrap_viz' #else --output_bootstrap_viz 'null' #end if #else --output_bootstrap_viz 'null' #end if
## Core Output Files --output_tsv '$output_tsv' --output_excel '$output_excel' --output_removed_seqs '$output_removed_seqs'
## Color Annotation Options #if $advanced_options.color_annotations_conditional.color_annotations #if $advanced_options.color_annotations_conditional.anno_type_conditional.annotation_type == 'FigTree' or $advanced_options.color_annotations_conditional.anno_type_conditional.annotation_type == 'both' --output_fig_tree '$output_fig_tree' #else --output_fig_tree 'null' #end if
#if $advanced_options.color_annotations_conditional.anno_type_conditional.annotation_type == 'iTol' or $advanced_options.color_annotations_conditional.anno_type_conditional.annotation_type == 'both' --output_itol '$output_itol' #else --output_itol 'null' #end if #else --output_fig_tree 'null' --output_itol 'null' #end if

param.name: input
param.label: Source ⚠️Unaligned FASTA file⚠️
param.help: A FASTA file containing any number of unaligned opsin amino-acid sequences.

param.name: prediction_prefix
param.label: Output file prefix
param.help: Specify a prefix for your output files. This will be used to name the datasets in your history.

param.name: model
param.label: Select prediction model
param.help: Models trained on more specific datasets (e.g., Vertebrate) may perform better if your data fits that category.

param.name: encoding
param.label: Select encoding method
param.help: Models trained on Amino-Acid Property Encoding perform slightly better but predictions take longer.

param.name: bootstrap
param.label: Enable bootstrap predictions?
param.help: Generates confidence intervals for predictions. This is highly recommended but is computationally intensive.

param.name: save_viz_as
param.label: Plot output format.
param.help: For now, we only are outputing compiled PDFs of PNGs due to Galaxy limitations...

param.name: full_spectrum_xaxis
param.label: Use full spectrum (300-650nm) x-axis?
param.help: If 'No' (default), the plot's x-axis will be scaled to fit the predictions. If 'Yes', it will span the full 300-650nm range.

param.name: blastp
param.label: Run BLASTp analysis against model dataset?

param.name: refseq
param.label: Reference sequence for BLASTp
param.help: Select a standard reference or provide your own.

param.name: ref_seq_file
param.label: Source file for custom reference sequence. *SHOULD CONTAIN 1 SEQUENCE* - Otherwise first sequence in file will be used.

section.name: advanced_options
section.title: Click here for advanced options: [For most people, you can just leave these as their default values]

param.name: tolerate_non_standard_aa
param.label: Tolerate non-standard amino acids (X, B, Z, etc.)?
param.help: If 'Yes', non-standard amino acids will be removed and sequences will be processed. If 'No', sequences containing them will be skipped.

param.name: n_jobs
param.label: Number of parallel processes (CPUs) to use
param.help: We limit this from 1-10 processes since we are running a public server.

param.name: color_annotations
param.label: Return iTol and/or FigTree tree-tip color annotations for opsin sequences? (Colors correspond to the predicted λmax converted to closest hex-code)

tool.help:
**What it does**
This Galaxy tool predicts the Opsin Phenotype (λmax, the wavelength of maximum absorbance) from an input file of UNALIGNED opsin amino-acid sequences in FASTA format, using the OPTICS prediction models.
-----
⚠️ **Public Server Resource Warning** ⚠️
This public Galaxy server has limited computing resources. OPTICS, especially with **bootstrap predictions** enabled, can be computationally intensive.
If you plan to analyze a large number of sequences:
1. Please be considerate and use the **"Number of parallel processes"** option to set a reasonable CPU limit (e.g. 5-7) instead of using the maximum.
2. For very large jobs, please consider downloading the command-line or GUI version of OPTICS to run locally or on your own cluster (https://github.com/VisualPhysiologyDB/optics).
-----
**Inputs**
* **Unaligned opsin FASTA file:** A FASTA file containing one or more unaligned opsin amino-acid sequences. * **Prediction Model:** Select the model trained on the dataset most relevant to your sequences. The 'whole-dataset' models are good general-purpose choices. 'MNM' models include additional physiological data and may be more accurate. * **Encoding Method:** 'Amino-Acid Property' is slightly more accurate but slower. 'One-Hot' is faster. * **Tolerate non-standard AAs:** If 'Yes' (default), non-standard amino acids (X, B, Z) are stripped. If 'No', sequences containing them are skipped. * **BLASTp analysis:** If 'Yes', runs BLASTp for each sequence against the model's training dataset to find the nearest neighbor and its percent identity. * **Bootstrap predictions:** If 'Yes', runs multiple bootstrap-resampled models to generate confidence intervals (e.g., 95% CI) for each prediction. This is highly recommended for robust analysis but is much slower. * **Bootstrap visualization:** If 'Yes', generates a PDF, PNG, or SVG plot visualizing the bootstrap prediction distributions.
-----
**Outputs**
The tool generates the following outputs:
* **OPTICS Predictions (TSV):** The main results file with λmax predictions. Includes bootstrap confidence intervals if enabled. * **OPTICS Predictions for Excel (XLSX):** The same results, formatted with λmax-to-color highlighting for easy viewing in Excel. * **BLASTp Report (CSV):** (Optional) A CSV file showing BLASTp results for each query sequence. * **Bootstrap Visualization (PDF/PNG/SVG):** (Optional) A plot showing the distribution of bootstrap predictions for each sequence. * **Removed Sequences Log (TXT):** A list of any sequence IDs that were skipped due to length constraints or non-standard amino acids. * **FigTree/iTOL Color Annotations (TXT):** Helper files to easily color-code your sequences by predicted λmax in phylogenetic tree viewers.
-----
**Example FASTA Entry**
>NP_001014890.1_rhodopsin_Bos taurus
MNGTEGPNFYVPFSNKTGVVRSPFEAPQYYLAEPWQFSMLAAYMFLLIMLGFPINFLTLYVTVQHKKLRT PLNYILLNLAVADLFMVFGGFTTTLYTSLHGYFVFGPTGCNLEGFFATLGGEIALWSLVVLAIERYVVVC KPMSNFRFGENHAIMGVAFTWVMALACAAPPLVGWSRYIPEGMQCSCGIDYYTPHEETNNESFVIYMFVV HFIIPLIVIFFCYGQLVFTVKEAAAQQQESATTQKAEKEVTRMVIIMVIAFLICWLPYAGVAFYIFTHQG SDFGPIFMTIPAFFAKTSAVYNPVIYIMMNKQFRNCMVTTLCCGKNPLGDDEASTTVSKTETSQVAPA
-----
**Additional Information**
This version of OPTICS (v1.3) uses the Visual Physiology Opsin Database (VPOD) v1.3 for training.
* VPOD GitHub: For those interested in VPOD and our pipeline for training ML models, visit its dedicated GitHub: https://github.com/VisualPhysiologyDB/visual-physiology-opsin-db * OPTICS Github: For those interested in using OPTICS locally, visit our other dedicated GitHub: https://github.com/VisualPhysiologyDB/optics

citations.citation: @article{10.1093/gigascience/giae073, author = {Frazer, Seth A and Baghbanzadeh, Mahdi and Rahnavard, Ali and Crandall, Keith A and Oakley, Todd H}, title = {Discovering genotype–phenotype relationships with machine learning and the Visual Physiology Opsin Database (VPOD)}, journal = {GigaScience}, volume = {13}, pages = {giae073}, year = {2024}, month = {10}, abstract = {Predicting phenotypes from genetic variation is foundational for fields as diverse as bioengineering and global change biology, highlighting the importance of efficient methods to predict gene functions. Linking genetic changes to phenotypic changes has been a goal of decades of experimental work, especially for some model gene families, including light-sensitive opsin proteins. Opsins can be expressed in vitro to measure light absorption parameters, including λmax—the wavelength of maximum absorbance—which strongly affects organismal phenotypes like color vision. Despite extensive research on opsins, the data remain dispersed, uncompiled, and often challenging to access, thereby precluding systematic and comprehensive analyses of the intricate relationships between genotype and phenotype.Here, we report a newly compiled database of all heterologously expressed opsin genes with λmax phenotypes that we call the Visual Physiology Opsin Database (VPOD). VPOD\_1.0 contains 864 unique opsin genotypes and corresponding λmax phenotypes collected across all animals from 73 separate publications. We use VPOD data and deepBreaks to show regression-based machine learning (ML) models often reliably predict λmax, account for nonadditive effects of mutations on function, and identify functionally critical amino acid sites.The ability to reliably predict functions from gene sequences alone using ML will allow robust exploration of molecular-evolutionary patterns governing phenotype, will inform functional and evolutionary connections to an organism’s ecological niche, and may be used more broadly for de novo protein design. Together, our database, phenotype predictions, and model comparisons lay the groundwork for future research applicable to families of genes with quantifiable and comparable phenotypes.}, issn = {2047-217X}, doi = {10.1093/gigascience/giae073}, url = {https://doi.org/10.1093/gigascience/giae073}, eprint = {https://academic.oup.com/gigascience/article-pdf/doi/10.1093/gigascience/giae073/60122488/giae073.pdf}, }
